The proxy's storage was never re-initialized; the role assignments, asset addresses, etc. that Zoth set up at deployment must still be there."},"test_Phase4_AttackByRandomEOA_Reverts()":{"notice":"Belt-and-suspenders: same attack via a fresh attacker (not even the admin). The original Zoth attack used the admin EOA, but we want to verify defense holds for any attacker."},"test_Phase4_StateIntactAfterFailedAttack()":{"notice":"Verify that AFTER the failed attack, the proxy still points to our protected implementation. The attack didn't corrupt anything."},"test_Phase4_ZothAttackReplay_Reverts()":{"notice":"CRITICAL: replay the actual Zoth attack pattern. The attacker (compromised deployer EOA) calls upgradeToAndCall directly on the proxy with the malicious implementation. This is the exact transaction signature of the real exploit. Against our protected version, this MUST fail."},"test_Phase5_LegitimateSignerCanStillUpgrade()":{"notice":"Demonstrate that the protected version still allows legitimate upgrades. The legitimate signer is the same EOA as the attacker (the Zoth deployer holds ADMIN_ROLE), but they have access to the chain seed.  We use a fresh USD0PPSubVaultProtectedV2 as the upgrade target. We import it inline in this test rather than at the top of the file to keep the imports minimal for the etch demo."}},"notice":"Phase 3: replace Zoth's deployed implementation bytecode with our         protected version, write the HMAC chain commitment to the proxy,         and verify the proxy retains all its original state while now         running our protected returns.excludedArtifacts_: The list of excluded artifact identifiers.

returns.excludedContracts_: The list of excluded contract addresses.

returns.excludedSelectors_: The list of excluded selector configurations.

returns.excludedSenders_: The list of excluded sender addresses.

returns._0: True if any assertion has failed, false otherwise.

returns.targetedArtifactSelectors_: The list of targeted artifact-selector configurations.

returns.targetedArtifacts_: The list of targeted artifact identifiers.

returns.targetedContracts_: The list of targeted contract addresses.

returns.targetedInterfaces_: The list of targeted address-interface configurations.

returns.targetedSelectors_: The list of targeted selector configurations.

returns.targetedSenders_: The list of targeted sender addresses.

failed().notice: Returns true if any test assertion has failed.

targetSenders().notice: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_Etch_ChainPositionInitiallyZero().notice: Verify the chain position is initialized to zero (default value, never written).

test_Etch_HmacCommitmentSet().notice: Verify our HMAC commitment is now set in the proxy storage.

test_Etch_ProtectedCodeAtZothImpl().notice: Verify our protected logic now runs at Zoth's implementation address.

test_Etch_ZothStatePreserved_AdminRole().notice: CRITICAL: verify Zoth's pre-existing state is preserved. The proxy's storage was never re-initialized; the role assignments, asset addresses, etc. that Zoth set up at deployment must still be there.

test_Phase4_AttackByRandomEOA_Reverts().notice: Belt-and-suspenders: same attack via a fresh attacker (not even the admin). The original Zoth attack used the admin EOA, but we want to verify defense holds for any attacker.

test_Phase4_StateIntactAfterFailedAttack.notice: Verify that AFTER the failed attack, the proxy still points to our protected implementation. The attack didn't corrupt anything.

test_Phase4_ZothAttackReplay_Reverts().notice: CRITICAL: replay the actual Zoth attack pattern. The attacker (compromised deployer EOA) calls upgradeToAndCall directly on the proxy with the malicious implementation. This is the exact transaction signature of the real exploit. Against our protected version, this MUST fail.

test_Phase5_LegitimateSignerCanStillUpgr.notice: Demonstrate that the protected version still allows legitimate upgrades. The legitimate signer is the same EOA as the attacker (the Zoth deployer holds ADMIN_ROLE), but they have access to the chain seed.  We use a fresh USD0PPSubVaultProtectedV2 as the upgrade target. We import it inline in this test rather than at the top of the file to keep the imports minimal for the etch demo.
